bikes.year_notes: US market entry May 2018, not 2019 as v1 stated. Year log per Electric Cycle Rider '5 Years With The Surron Light Bee X' (2024, corroborated by Ronhack): 2019 sealed chain/bearings/footpegs; 2020 cable throttle replaced by electronic throttle, OEM chain box; 2021 dual-hall throttle, thicker brake discs, KKE front/rear suspension adopted (mixed with DNM by production availability), Gates primary belt, battery to 34Ah; 2022 power 5kW to 6kW, battery 38.5Ah, new stem/front fender; 2023 battery 40Ah. Factory Minibikes tech page adds a pre/post-May-2019 speed-limiter wiring difference (green/black wire loop behavior flips). KKE aftermarket forks list fitment as 2019-2023, and Sur-Ron itself sells a 'Light Bee KKE Front Fork' as OEM, confirming the dual-supplier era. US-market bikes were still commonly listed with 32Ah packs into 2021-2022, so verify the pack label at intake, not the model year.
bikes.blurb: The default halo e-moto. Every owner strips it in month one, so takeoff supply is bottomless and resale is slow.

stock_parts.comp_note: Stock rear shock on 2018-2020 Light Bees (ECR year log: KKE rear adopted 2021, though DNM still appears in some later runs). Same physical part as the Segway X260 shock so the pool merges; catalog_name kept unchanged. New RCP-2S retails $165 at Luna. Takeoff pulls saturate r/Surron and eBay and sit unsold; no confirmed sold prices visible, so pegged well under half of new.

upgrade.name: Fastace BDA53RC rear shock
upgrade.note: First thing everyone bins. The stock DNM is the fuse in the system, this one actually damps.

stock_parts.catalog_name: Sur-Ron Light Bee KKE stock rear shock (2021-2025)
stock_parts.comp_note: 2021+ production adopted KKE rear suspension per ECR year log, but supply mixed with DNM by production run, so identify the unit on the shock body at intake, not by year alone. Same upgrade culture as the DNM era; no sold comps found, pegged low.

upgrade.name: Fastace BDA53RC rear shock
upgrade.note: Same swap regardless of which OEM shock the bike shipped with.

stock_parts.catalog_name: Sur-Ron Light Bee stock inverted fork (DNM takeoff)
stock_parts.comp_note: The DNM USD-8 is the 2018-2020 stock fork (ECR: KKE adopted 2021). Fork upgrades (Fastace, KKE) are the most common LBX mod, so stock fork takeoffs are everywhere. Retail replacements run $300 plus but used pulls move slow; pegged low. Catalog name kept from v1 since it already identifies the DNM part.

upgrade.name: Fastace ALX13RC 2.0 fork
upgrade.note: The standard fork swap. Stock DNM legs flex and blow seals when you jump the thing.

stock_parts.catalog_name: Sur-Ron Light Bee KKE stock fork (2021-2025)
stock_parts.comp_note: KKE became an OEM fork supplier from 2021 (ECR year log; Sur-Ron's own store sells the 'Light Bee KKE Front Fork'; elecycles cross-lists KKE/DNM stock forks). Production mixed KKE and DNM by availability, so verify the brand stamped on the lowers at intake. Slightly stronger resale than the DNM era fork; still no confirmed solds, pegged low.

upgrade.name: Fastace ALX13RC 2.0 fork
upgrade.note: Jumpers still swap the KKE-era fork; better than the old DNM but not by enough.

stock_parts.comp_note: 5kW-era controller, cross-compatible with Segway X260 per eBay/AIC Motorsports listings, so the pools merge and the shared catalog_name is kept. Note the pre/post-May-2019 limiter wiring difference (Factory Minibikes): wire-loop behavior flips, worth flagging in the listing. Asks cluster $100-200; sold prices assumed lower on saturated supply.

upgrade.name: EBMX X-9000 V3 controller kit
upgrade.note: The big power play. Stock controller comes off the day the EBMX box lands.

stock_parts.comp_note: Power rating rose from 5kW to 6kW with the 2022 model year (ECR year log), which is a controller/motor re-rate, so 2022+ controllers are split from the earlier pool. Connector revisions across runs are reported in the community; photograph plugs at intake. No confirmed solds; pegged low against the same saturated-supply dynamics.

upgrade.name: EBMX X-9000 V3 controller kit
upgrade.note: Same story in the 6kW era. Stock unit lands in a drawer.

stock_parts.comp_note: No evidence of a tire spec change across the production run; CST 19in knobbies throughout. Street conversions pull them with near-zero wear. Cheap new (about $35 each), so used pairs fetch little. No sold comps found, pegged low.

upgrade.name: Shinko 241 street tire pair
upgrade.note: The go-to street conversion tire. Stock knobbies squirm on pavement.

stock_parts.comp_note: The classic 32Ah pack (176 Panasonic cells per Factory Minibikes). Nominally 2018-2020, but US-market bikes commonly shipped 32Ah into 2021-2022, so ALWAYS price off the pack label, not the frame year. New OEM packs run $800 eBay to $1,599 dealer; used guidance is 40-60 percent under retail. Cycle count unverifiable at intake, pegged conservative.

upgrade.name: Amorge 72V 35Ah conversion battery
upgrade.note: The 72V conversion crowd pulls the stock pack while it still holds a charge.

stock_parts.comp_note: ECR year log: capacity stepped to 34Ah in 2021 then 38.5Ah in 2022, with market/region overlap (transitional 34Ah packs exist and some US 2021-22 units still carried 32Ah). Verify the printed Ah on the pack at intake. Higher capacity holds a modest premium over the 32Ah pool; no confirmed solds, pegged conservative.

upgrade.name: Amorge 72V 35Ah conversion battery
upgrade.note: Same 72V conversion path frees these packs up healthy.

stock_parts.comp_note: Third capacity bump per ECR year log; 40Ah is the pack listed on current Light Bee X retail pages. Newest and most desirable stock pack, still discounts hard used on unverifiable health. No confirmed solds; base pegged at 40 percent of a conservative comp.

upgrade.name: Amorge 72V 35Ah conversion battery
upgrade.note: Even 40Ah owners chasing 72V power pull these while fresh.

stock_parts.comp_note: No evidence of a seat change across years. Tall and comfort seat swaps are common; stock plank seats pile up. No sold comps found, low ask assumed.

upgrade.name: Charged Cycle Works tall comfort seat
upgrade.note: Stock seat is a 2x4 with vinyl on it. Taller riders swap it week one.

stock_parts.catalog_name: Sur-Ron 48T stock rear sprocket and chain (takeoff)
stock_parts.comp_note: 48T/420 spec constant across the run (Factory Minibikes). 2019 added a sealed chain but same spec, so no split. Wear items discount hard used; pegged low, no sold comps found.

upgrade.name: GritShift 54T aluminum rear sprocket
upgrade.note: Torque gearing for trail riders. Steel stocker comes off half worn.

stock_parts.comp_note: Pegs were 'improved' in 2019 per ECR but remained small cast units the community still bins, so one pool. Nearly giveaway items in group sales; no individual sold comps found.

upgrade.name: Warp 9 billet footpegs
upgrade.note: Stock pegs are small and slick. Billet pegs are the cheapest confidence upgrade on the bike.

bikes.motor: Mid-mounted PMSM: 12.5kW peak (2023-2024); V2/HP hairpin-wound motor 18kW sustained, 21kW peak (2025)
bikes.year_notes: Two real generations plus one mid-year change. Launch spec (2023): 12.5kW peak, 74V 55Ah, 19in wheels both ends. Mid-2024: rear wheel switched from 19in to 18in during the model year (Cycle News 2025 Ultra Bee HP review), so 2024 bikes exist both ways. 2025 V2/HP: hairpin-wound motor at 18kW sustained/21kW peak with Turbo and Crawl modes, 74V 60Ah pack, upgraded suspension and brakes (Cycle News; GritShift 'Ultra Bee V2 Tested By Pro Riders'). V2 parts do not merge with 2023-2024 pools.
bikes.blurb: The grown-up Sur-Ron. Better stock parts than the Light Bee but owners still chase Sirris suspension and EBMX power, so takeoffs flow steadily.

stock_parts.catalog_name: Sur-Ron Ultra Bee stock rear shock (takeoff)
stock_parts.comp_note: Gen-1 (2023-2024) shock; 2025 V2 got revised suspension so pools are split. Sirris R46 is the recommended swap per Electric Cycle Rider and Darwin EV, so stock shocks come off constantly. No sold comps visible; pegged low against thick supply.

upgrade.name: Sirris R46 rear shock
upgrade.note: Purpose-tuned for the Ultra Bee. Stock shock fades once you ride it like the ads show.

stock_parts.stock_part: Stock Ultra Bee V2 rear shock (revised 2025 spec)
stock_parts.catalog_name: Sur-Ron Ultra Bee V2 rear shock (2025)
stock_parts.comp_note: 2025 V2/HP suspension was 'greatly improved' over gen-1 per Cycle News, so this is a distinct part with fewer reasons to come off. Model too new for any resale evidence; floor value only.

stock_parts.stock_part: Stock Ultra Bee inverted fork (9.4in travel)
stock_parts.catalog_name: Sur-Ron Ultra Bee stock inverted fork (takeoff)
stock_parts.comp_note: Gen-1 fork; 2025 V2 suspension revision splits the pool. Stock fork is decent so swap rate is lower than the Light Bee, but Sirris F43 and KKE 37mm conversions still generate takeoffs. No sold comps found.

upgrade.name: Sirris F43 fork
upgrade.note: More travel and real damping for riders who actually jump the bike.

stock_parts.stock_part: Stock Ultra Bee V2 inverted fork (revised 2025 spec)
stock_parts.comp_note: Upgraded for 2025 per Cycle News, so fewer swaps expected and no takeoff market exists yet. Floor value only.

Gen-1 12.5kW controller. The 2025 V2/HP runs a different controller (18kW sustained, Turbo/Crawl modes) and does not merge. One pre-owned eBay listing at $299 ask, not a confirmed sale; pegged well under the ask.

upgrade.name: EBMX X-9000 V3 controller kit
upgrade.note: Same story as every Sur-Ron. Stock FOC unit is fine until the owner wants 50kW.

stock_parts.stock_part: Stock V2/HP controller (18kW sustained, Turbo/Crawl modes)
stock_parts.comp_note: New 2025 unit powering the hairpin motor (Cycle News, GritShift V2 review). Strong enough that swap pressure is lower; no resale market exists yet. Floor value only.

stock_parts.stock_part: Stock 19in front and rear moto knobby pair
stock_parts.catalog_name: Sur-Ron Ultra Bee 19in knobby tire pair (takeoff)
stock_parts.comp_note: 19/19 wheel setup applies to 2023 through mid-2024 only: Sur-Ron switched the rear to 18in DURING the 2024 model year (Cycle News), so check the rear rim stamp on any 2024 bike. Supermoto and MX tire swaps are routine; used OEM knobbies move cheap, no sold comps found.

upgrade.name: Shinko 505 Cheater tire set
upgrade.note: The trail-bite upgrade. Stock rubber is a compromise compound.

stock_parts.stock_part: Stock 19in front / 18in rear moto knobby set
stock_parts.catalog_name: Sur-Ron Ultra Bee 19F/18R knobby tire set (2024-2025)
stock_parts.comp_note: Late-2024 onward runs the 18in rear for better tire selection and off-road performance (Cycle News). Different rear size means these do NOT pool with the 19/19 pairs. Same cheap-used dynamics; no sold comps found.

upgrade.name: Shinko 505 Cheater tire set
upgrade.note: 18in rear opens up real moto rubber choices; stock set still comes off.

stock_parts.comp_note: No evidence of a seat change across 2023-2025 (V2 chassis 'largely unchanged' per Cycle News). Tall seat swaps common for adult riders. No sold comps found, estimate kept low.

upgrade.name: Charged Cycle Works tall comfort seat
upgrade.note: Adds standover comfort for full-size riders on long trail days.

stock_parts.catalog_name: Sur-Ron Ultra Bee stock rear sprocket and chain (takeoff)
stock_parts.comp_note: No evidence of a drivetrain spec change; 2025 HP got a heavier-duty chain on the Talaria-competitor MX5, not here. Wear item with cheap new replacements. No sold comps found; pegged low.

upgrade.name: Warp 9 aluminum rear sprocket
upgrade.note: Lighter 7075 aluminum and gearing choice in one swap.

stock_parts.comp_note: No evidence of a peg change across years. Billet peg swaps near universal on ridden bikes. No individual sold comps found.

upgrade.name: GritShift billet footpegs
upgrade.note: Wider platform, actual grip. Stock pegs go in the parts bin.

bikes.year_notes: Corrections: v1 listed '96V 48Ah' and years 2021-2025. The 96V figure came from pre-production press; delivered bikes run a 90V-nominal 48Ah pack, 4.3kWh (Electric Cycle Rider's Sept 2022 range test of a production bike; Flash E-Bikes sells the OEM pack as 'Storm Bee 90V 48Ah Sony battery'). Bike was unveiled in 2020 but meaningful deliveries started 2022, so years corrected to 2022-2025. Some sources (Mudbike) claim a later '104V 55Ah' production pack; unverified in the wild and possibly a max-charge-voltage/nominal confusion, so no year split; verify the pack label on any intake. No other verified running changes; US units are grey-market and thin.
bikes.blurb: Full-size 90V flagship, still in production but thin on the ground in the US. Takeoff parts exist in small numbers and resale evidence is nearly nonexistent.

stock_parts.catalog_name: Sur-Ron Storm Bee stock rear shock (takeoff)
stock_parts.comp_note: No evidence of year-to-year change. Very few US bikes so almost no takeoff sales found. Pegged low on thin evidence and a tiny buyer pool.

stock_parts.catalog_name: Sur-Ron Storm Bee stock inverted fork (takeoff)
stock_parts.comp_note: No evidence of year-to-year change. Full-size fork with moto-crossover value but no comps found for used Storm Bee pulls. Conservative estimate for a tiny market.
System is 90V nominal (v1's '96V' label kept in catalog_name for DB merge; description corrected). No resale sales found anywhere indexed. No realistic exit price, so floor value only.

stock_parts.catalog_name: Sur-Ron Storm Bee 21/18 knobby tire pair (takeoff)
stock_parts.comp_note: No evidence of change. Full-size moto tire sizes at least have a broad dirtbike buyer pool. Still no Storm Bee specific comps found; used knobby pairs are cheap everywhere.

upgrade.name: Shinko 546 front and 505 rear tire set
upgrade.note: Standard full-size dirtbike rubber. Stock tires come off for terrain-specific sets.

stock_parts.comp_note: No used market found. Floor value only.

stock_parts.catalog_name: Sur-Ron Storm Bee stock rear sprocket and chain (takeoff)
stock_parts.comp_note: Wear item, no used market found. Floor value only.

bikes.year_notes: Years and specs verified: MX4 (sold as 'Sting R' at launch) released 2023 with 8kW IPM motor, 60V 45Ah pack, 220mm rotors (GritShift MX4 deep dive; pitbikeusa comparisons). CRITICAL intake distinction: the 2022 Sting MX3 is a DIFFERENT bike (6kW PMSM, 60V 38.4Ah, 203mm rotors) sharing the frame but with non-interchangeable motor and battery and different headlight/kill-switch connectors (GritShift, Talaria Canada) - MX3 parts must not be listed against this entry. The MX5 (72V 40Ah, 13.4kW) is a later separate model, not an MX4 year variant. No verified running changes WITHIN the MX4's own 2023-2025 run, so no year splits.
bikes.blurb: Sting MX4 and Sting R MX4 are the same bike per GritShift and Talaria dealers, so one entry covers both names. Sur-Ron's main rival with the same strip-it-day-one owner culture.

stock_parts.catalog_name: Talaria Sting MX4 factory rear shock (takeoff)
stock_parts.comp_note: Suspension upgrades are the top Talaria mod category at every retailer, so stock shocks flood out. No confirmed sold prices found; pegged low. MX3 suspension components physically similar but keep pools separate on connector-era caution.

upgrade.name: Fastace BDA53RC rear shock
upgrade.note: Same swap as the Sur-Ron crowd makes. Stock shock is serviceable, this is better everywhere.

stock_parts.comp_note: New factory fork 2.0 retails $699 at Luna but used takeoffs are plentiful and slow movers. No sold comps found; pegged far under new. No year-variant evidence within MX4.

upgrade.name: KKE 37mm front fork kit
upgrade.note: Stiffer legs for riders who case jumps. The factory fork is fine right up until it is not.

stock_parts.comp_note: MX4-specific 8kW controller; NOT interchangeable with MX3 (different motor tech and connectors per GritShift). EBMX sells a plug-and-play MX4 kit so pulls are common. No sold comps visible; pegged low against saturated supply.

upgrade.name: EBMX X-9000 V3 controller kit
upgrade.note: Plug-and-play on the MX4. Stock controller comes off working and lands in a drawer.

stock_parts.catalog_name: Talaria Sting 19in CST knobby tire pair (takeoff)
stock_parts.comp_note: Same saturation story as Sur-Ron. Cheap new, cheaper used, street converters pull them nearly unworn. No year-variant evidence.

upgrade.name: Shinko 241 street tire pair
upgrade.note: Supermoto conversion staple for pavement-only Stings.

stock_parts.comp_note: 45Ah is MX4-only; the MX3's 38.4Ah pack is a different, non-interchangeable part (GritShift) - check the label. New spare packs run about $1,500 at Luna. Used pack pricing tracks 50 to 60 percent under new with unverifiable cycle counts; pegged conservative.

upgrade.name: eWatt 60V 53Ah battery
upgrade.note: Range chasers step up capacity and sell the stock pack while it still tests healthy.

stock_parts.comp_note: Tall seat swaps common. No sold comps found; low estimate. Seat/plastics shared across Sting generations per GritShift, so this pool can absorb MX3 seats.

upgrade.name: Charged Cycle Works tall comfort seat
upgrade.note: Stock foam packs out fast. Taller riders swap early.

stock_parts.catalog_name: Talaria Sting stock rear sprocket and chain (takeoff)
stock_parts.comp_note: Wear item with cheap new replacements. Pegged low, no sold comps found.

upgrade.name: GritShift aluminum rear sprocket
upgrade.note: Gearing swap for trail torque. Stock steel ring is heavy.

stock_parts.comp_note: Universal billet peg swap culture. Stock pegs are giveaway items in bundle sales. Shared across Sting generations.

upgrade.name: GritShift billet footpegs
upgrade.note: Cheap, grippy, and the stock pegs deserve the bin.

bikes.motor: 6kW peak mid-mounted IPM with belt primary
bikes.year_notes: Launch dating is muddy: US dealer pages date the 'X3 Concept' to Feb 2025, while US market chatter has bikes circulating from 2024; keeping v1's 2024-2025 with low confidence. Verified specs: 6kW peak IPM, 60V 40Ah LG 21700 M50 pack, 17in supermoto wheels (70/90-17 front on 1.6in rim, 90/80-17 rear on 2.15in rim), 4-piston hydraulic brakes with 220mm rotors, belt primary (Talaria dealer spec pages). Note there is also a higher-spec 'X3 Pro' variant - treat its parts separately at intake, do not merge. No verified running changes; model too new; no year splits.
bikes.blurb: Talaria's 17in supermoto pit bike, sold as X3 or xXx. Newer and lower volume than the Sting, so the used parts market is thin and prices must stay careful.

stock_parts.comp_note: Model is new and low volume; no used comps found. Priced off Sting shock behavior with a further discount for the smaller buyer pool.
No used comps found for X3 forks. Conservative estimate against a thin market.
No resale market found for X3 controllers yet. Floor value only.
Sizes verified against dealer spec pages (70/90-17 front, 90/80-17 rear). 17in moto sizes have some crossover demand but no X3 specific comps found. Pegged low.

upgrade.name: Michelin Pilot Street 2 tire pair
upgrade.note: Real motorcycle rubber for a bike that gets ridden like a motorcycle.

stock_parts.comp_note: No used market found. Floor value only.
No used market found. Floor value only.

upgrade.name: ProTaper micro handlebar
upgrade.note: Standard bar swap for fit and crash resistance.

bikes.year_notes: Years corrected from 2020-2025: the X260 is discontinued - by 2025-2026 resellers list it as discontinued/replaced, with new-unit availability drying up around 2023; Segway is teasing a NEW dirt-bike line for CES 2026, which is a different platform. Factory Minibikes notes 2020-2021 production carried 'mixed specifications requiring case-by-case inspection' (app-lock/limiter behavior varies by run), but no VERIFIED component change was found, so no year-variant splits - inspect controller plugs and app-pairing behavior at intake instead. Controller and rear shock pool with the 5kW-era Sur-Ron parts.
bikes.blurb: Sur-Ron derived platform, and many parts physically interchange with the Light Bee X. Same upgrade culture, same flooded takeoff market.

stock_parts.comp_note: Same DNM unit as the 2018-2020 Light Bee X per ebikezilla cross-listing; merges with the Sur-Ron takeoff pool (Segway kept DNM through its run - no evidence it followed Sur-Ron's 2021 KKE switch). New retails $165; used pulls saturate the market.

upgrade.name: Fastace BDA53RC rear shock
upgrade.note: Identical swap path to the Sur-Ron. Stock DNM is the weak link.

stock_parts.comp_note: KKE sells a $799 X160/X260 fork kit, so stock fork pulls are routine. No sold comps found; pegged low. No year-variant evidence.

upgrade.name: KKE 37mm front fork kit
upgrade.note: The proven X260 fork fix for riders over 175 pounds.

stock_parts.comp_note: eBay and AIC Motorsports market these interchangeably with 5kW-era Light Bee X controllers, so the pools merge (the X260 never got Sur-Ron's 2022 6kW re-rate). X260 adds Bluetooth app pairing - note pairing status in listings. Asks $100 to $200, no visible solds; pegged low.

upgrade.name: EBMX X-9000 V3 controller kit
upgrade.note: EBMX supports the Segway platform. Stock unit comes off in an afternoon.

stock_parts.comp_note: Shared size and pattern with the Sur-Ron pool. Cheap new, near worthless used unless unridden.

upgrade.name: Shinko 241 street tire pair
upgrade.note: Street conversion standard.

stock_parts.comp_note: Same class as Sur-Ron packs but a smaller buyer pool since Segway sold fewer bikes; discontinuation cuts both ways (fewer buyers, but replacement packs getting scarce may firm prices). Pegged slightly under the Sur-Ron pack; no confirmed solds.

upgrade.name: Amorge 72V 35Ah conversion battery
upgrade.note: 72V conversions run the same play here as on the Light Bee.

stock_parts.comp_note: Tall seat swaps common. No sold comps found; low estimate.

upgrade.name: Charged Cycle Works tall comfort seat
upgrade.note: Same plank-seat complaint as the Sur-Ron, same fix.

stock_parts.catalog_name: Segway X260 stock rear sprocket and chain (takeoff)
stock_parts.comp_note: Wear item, cheap new. Pegged low, no sold comps found.

upgrade.name: GritShift aluminum rear sprocket
upgrade.note: GritShift carries a full X260 line. Gearing swap is the usual first drivetrain mod.

bikes.year_notes: Years corrected from 2020-2025 for the same reason as the X260: line discontinued, new-unit availability dried up around 2023 (2025-2026 reseller status pages). Same 2020-2021 mixed-spec caveat from Factory Minibikes, no verified component change, so no year splits.
bikes.blurb: The X260's smaller sibling, big with the teen rider crowd. Parts demand exists but skews cheap, and the buyer pool is smaller than the 60V bikes.

stock_parts.catalog_name: DNM RCP-2S 9.5in short rear shock (Segway X160 takeoff)
stock_parts.comp_note: Luna sells the short RCP-2S new for $165 as the X160 and Sur-Ron S replacement. Used demand is thinner than the 10.5in version; pegged low.
KKE fork kit fits X160 per KKE listing, so pulls happen. No sold comps found; low estimate.

upgrade.name: KKE 37mm front fork kit
upgrade.note: Heavier riders on the X160 outgun the stock fork fast.

stock_parts.comp_note: REVRides carries aftermarket X160 controllers so stock pulls exist. No sold comps found; pegged low.
Small-size knobbies, cheap new. Low estimate, no comps found.

upgrade.name: Shinko 241 street tire pair
upgrade.note: 241s come in 17in sizes and turn the X160 into a pavement bike.

stock_parts.comp_note: No used market found. Floor value only.

stock_parts.catalog_name: Segway X160 stock rear sprocket and chain (takeoff)
stock_parts.comp_note: Wear item, no used market found. Floor value only.

bikes.year_notes: Years corrected from 2020-2025: the R-series (R and RX) launched Feb 2020 (Electrek Jan 25 2020; Motorcyclist 2020 review), and the RX is absent from the MY24 lineup announcement - Super73's MY24 R-series is the 'R Adventure'. Last new RX model year is 2023 (2023 RXs on Cycle Trader; no MY24 RX exists). No verified running changes within 2020-2023 (trim colorways like Mojave are cosmetic), so no year splits.
bikes.blurb: The flagship Super73 with full adjustable suspension. Owners customize hard, but the parts culture is cosmetic and comfort driven rather than performance teardown.

stock_parts.catalog_name: Super73 RX stock piggyback coilover shock (takeoff)
stock_parts.comp_note: Custom builds documented swapping to Fox Float rear shocks, so takeoffs exist. No sold comps found; pegged low.

upgrade.name: Fox Float X rear shock
upgrade.note: The documented RX ride-quality fix. Stock coilover is springy, not plush.

stock_parts.stock_part: Stock RX inverted air-assist coil fork
stock_parts.comp_note: Stock RX fork is adjustable and usually retained even in custom builds per build threads. Few takeoffs, few buyers; low estimate.
Tire swaps between GRZLY and BDGR patterns are the most common Super73 mod. New BDGRs run about $80 each, capping used takeoff pairs low.

upgrade.name: Super73 BDGR Override 20x4.5 tire pair
upgrade.note: Street riders swap the knobby GRZLYs for BDGR pavement tread.

stock_parts.comp_note: Seat swaps for two-up pads and custom benches are common in the Super73 scene. No sold comps found; low estimate.
Bar swaps happen for fit and style. Cheap part, low resale.

stock_parts.stock_part: Stock Super73 display and thumb throttle
stock_parts.comp_note: Mostly sold as spares to owners with broken units rather than upgraders. Thin market, low estimate. RX ended before the MY24 throttle redesign, so all RX units are the legacy type.
Super73 sold the S2/R-Series pack across the lineup through MY23; ChiBattery upgrade buyers free up stock packs. Used e-bike batteries discount hard on unverifiable health; pegged conservative.

upgrade.name: ChiBattery Systems Ranger extended battery
upgrade.note: The known range fix for S2 and R-series bikes. Stock pack comes off healthy.

bikes.year_notes: Real MY24 break (announced fall 2023, Super73 MY24 press release): new throttle, PRO-series UL2271 battery replacing the 960Wh pack (press release lists '500 PRO' 500Wh for S2; the current S2 SE page shows 720Wh 48V 15Ah, so PRO capacity varies by trim/year - read the pack label), Tektro HD-E350 2-piston hydraulic brakes, and co-branded Maxxis SuperSucker tires (4.5in front, 5in rear) replacing the BDGR set. Pre-2024 S2s are the classic 960Wh/BDGR spec. Fork correction: current Super73 spec sheets describe the S2 fork as an adjustable air-spring unit, not the coil fork v1 claimed; no evidence of a fork hardware change at MY24, so one fork row with corrected description.
bikes.blurb: The volume seller of the Super73 lineup. Big install base means steady takeoff flow, but parts are cheap new so used prices stay modest.

stock_parts.stock_part: Stock S2 adjustable air-spring suspension fork
stock_parts.comp_note: Renamed from v1's 'coil fork' - Super73 spec pages describe the S2 fork as a fully adjustable air-spring unit, and no evidence was found of a fork hardware change across years. Some owners upgrade for comfort; thin resale demand, low estimate.

BDGR is the pre-MY24 stock tire; from MY24 the S2 ships Maxxis SuperSuckers (Super73 MY24 press release), so BDGR takeoffs date the donor to 2020-2023. BDGR-GRZLY swaps are constant in the scene. About $80 each new caps the used pair price hard.

upgrade.name: Super73 GRZLY 20x5 tire pair
upgrade.note: The trail-look swap. Street BDGRs come off with plenty of tread left.

stock_parts.stock_part: Super73 x Maxxis SuperSucker pair (4.5in front, 5in rear)
stock_parts.comp_note: MY24+ stock tire, co-branded with Maxxis (MY24 press release; current S2 SE spec page confirms 4.5F/5R). Better street reputation than BDGR so swap pressure is lower; takeoffs exist from GRZLY converters. No sold comps found; pegged low.

upgrade.name: Super73 GRZLY 20x5 tire pair
upgrade.note: Knobby-look conversion still pulls fresh SuperSuckers off MY24 bikes.

stock_parts.comp_note: Bench and custom seat swaps common; no evidence of a seat hardware change (MY24 colorways add a brown seat option, cosmetic only). No sold comps found; low estimate.
Same bar across much of the lineup; merges with the RX pool. Low value part.

stock_parts.stock_part: Stock Super73 display and thumb throttle
stock_parts.comp_note: Legacy unit; MY24 introduced a redesigned throttle across the lineup (MY24 press release), so this pool is 2020-2023 donors on the S2. Spare-part demand only. Merges with the shared Super73 display pool.
New throttle design shipped on all MY24 models (press release). Not interchangeable listing-wise with the legacy unit. Too new for any resale evidence; floor value only.

The classic 960Wh pack, shared with the R-series and standard on S2 through MY23; replaced by the PRO-series packs at MY24 (press release). ChiBattery upgrade buyers free up stock packs. Health unverifiable at intake; pegged conservative.

upgrade.name: ChiBattery Systems Ranger extended battery
upgrade.note: Range upgrade of choice for S2 owners. Frees up a healthy stock pack.

stock_parts.stock_part: PRO-series 48V UL2271 pack (500Wh MY24 core; 720Wh 15Ah current S2 SE)
stock_parts.comp_note: MY24 tiered PRO packs replaced the 960Wh unit (press release); capacity varies by trim and year (500 PRO at MY24 launch, 720Wh on the current S2 SE per super73.com), so record the printed Wh at intake. Smaller than the legacy pack, so priced under it despite being newer. No sold comps found.

bikes.year_notes: Launch year 2022 VERIFIED (announced March 2022, available Spring 2022 as the Z1's successor - MUSE TV, Cleantechnica; v1 had this right). MY24 changes per Super73's MY24 press release and the current Z Miami Core page: new throttle design, 48V head/tail lights, and the battery moved from the original 615Wh to a 480Wh (48V 10Ah) UL2271 PRO pack. Tires are Super73 Trooper street tread throughout - no evidence of a tire change, description enriched only. Brakes remain Tektro 2-piston mechanical.
bikes.blurb: Entry-level Super73 with no suspension and a smaller pack. Least teardown culture in the lineup, so takeoff volume and values both run light.

stock_parts.comp_note: Trooper street tread confirmed on the current Z Miami Core page (4in front and rear); no evidence of a pattern change across years, so one pool, description enriched. Tire swaps are the main Z-series mod. Cheap new, capping used pairs low; no sold comps found.

upgrade.name: Super73 BDGR Override 20x4.5 tire pair
upgrade.note: Fresh tread and the big-block look for the commuter crowd.

stock_parts.catalog_name: Super73 Z Miami stock bench seat (takeoff)
stock_parts.comp_note: Custom seat swaps for style are common. Low value part, no comps found.
Cosmetic swaps only. Near-floor value.

Original launch pack (615Wh per InsideEVs spec); replaced at MY24 by the 480Wh PRO pack, so 615Wh units date the donor to 2022-2023. Less demand than the S2/R pack; pegged conservative with unverifiable health.
MY24+ pack per the current Z Miami Core spec page (480Wh, 48V 10Ah, UL2271). Smaller and newer; thin resale market, pegged conservative.
Legacy unit; MY24 Z-series got a redesigned throttle (MY24 press release), so this row is 2022-2023 donors. Spare-part demand only and Class 2 specific. No resale market found; floor value.
New throttle design across MY24 models (press release). Too new for resale evidence; floor value only.

bikes.year_notes: Years corrected from v1's 2021-2024: the R Brooklyn was a launch trim of the Feb-2020 R-series (Electrek Jan 25 2020; Motorcyclist 2020 R-series review) and was replaced by the R Adventure for MY24; 'Legacy Series' R Brooklyn SE pages after that are sell-through of the old platform, not new model years. All stock parts belong to the pre-MY24 legacy spec (960Wh pack, legacy throttle), so no intra-run splits needed.
bikes.blurb: Full-suspension R-series in the Brooklyn trim. Shares the S2/R parts bin, so most takeoffs merge into existing pools.

stock_parts.comp_note: Simpler shock than the RX piggyback unit. Swaps happen for ride quality; no sold comps found, low estimate.

upgrade.name: Fox Float X rear shock
upgrade.note: Same Fox swap the RX crowd runs. Stock unit is basic.

stock_parts.comp_note: Usually retained. Thin takeoff flow and thin demand; low estimate.
Street tread swapped for GRZLY knobbies and back constantly. Cheap new caps used pairs. R Brooklyn ended before the MY24 Maxxis switch, so all its takeoffs are BDGR-era.

upgrade.name: Super73 GRZLY 20x5 tire pair
upgrade.note: The dirt-look swap on the street bike.

stock_parts.comp_note: Merges with the RX saddle pool. Custom seat culture keeps takeoffs flowing; low value.
Same shared pack, same pool, same conservative peg on unverifiable health. R Brooklyn only ever shipped this pack (run ended before the MY24 PRO packs).

upgrade.name: ChiBattery Systems Ranger extended battery
upgrade.note: Range fix shared across the R and S2 platforms.

stock_parts.stock_part: Stock Super73 display and thumb throttle
stock_parts.comp_note: Merges with the shared legacy display pool (all R Brooklyns pre-date the MY24 throttle redesign). Spare-part demand only.

bikes.motor: 5.4kW rear hub motor (72V era); 80V hub motor on 2024+ RCR 80V
bikes.year_notes: Three battery eras, verified: launch pack was 72V 23Ah (Panasonic NCR18650PF, 20S8P - johnangel.nyc battery specifications); the 41Ah pack (200x 21700 cells, 20S10P) was announced Nov 16 2020 (Electrek 'ONYX RCR gets major updates and doubles range') and shipped on 2021+ orders, backwards compatible; the RCR 80V (45Ah, new controller with Thrill/Cruise modes and revised throttle mapping) launched 2024 (onyxmotors.com RCR 80V page; johnangel.nyc 80V explainer). Frame battery-tray width grew across revisions v1.0 to v1.2 to v1.5 (johnangel.nyc), so pack/tray fit is revision-dependent - record the frame version at intake. Company timeline: founder died 2021, brand relaunched with the limited RCR LTD in 2023 (thepack.news), then the 80V. Stock Kelly controller spans all 72V versions (v1.0 through v1.75 and LTD, per johnangel.nyc Sicko Mode notes); the 80V controller is a distinct part. No evidence of suspension/tire/seat spec changes across years.
bikes.blurb: Cafe-racer moped with real motorcycle speed. Loyal owner base mods steadily but the parts market is a fraction of the Sur-Ron world, so resale is slow and thin.

stock_parts.catalog_name: ONYX RCR stock 350mm rear shock pair (takeoff)
stock_parts.comp_note: No evidence of a shock spec change across revisions. ONYX sells upgraded rear shocks direct, so stock pairs come off. No sold comps found; pegged low for a small owner base.

upgrade.name: ONYX Motors upgraded RCR rear shocks
upgrade.note: Factory upgrade path. Stock shocks are budget units on a fast bike.

stock_parts.comp_note: Fork swaps less common than rear shocks. No comps found; conservative estimate. No year-variant evidence.
Dual sport tire swaps are the standard RCR mod. Used street takeoffs cheap; no sold comps found.

upgrade.name: Shinko 244 dual sport tire pair
upgrade.note: The RCR community default for mixed surface riding.

stock_parts.comp_note: Cafe seat swaps happen for style. Small market, occasional demand from crash repairs; low estimate.

The Kelly controller spans every 72V RCR revision (v1.0-v1.75 incl. LTD, per johnangel.nyc); community 'Sicko Mode' tuning runs on it. The 2024+ RCR 80V uses a different controller - do not pool. No resale sales found; floor value only.

Distinct 80V unit with new firmware (Thrill and Cruise modes, revised throttle mapping, display temperature readout - johnangel.nyc 80V explainer). Too new and too rare for any resale evidence; floor value only.

Launch-era pack (johnangel.nyc battery specifications). These 18650 packs are now 5+ years old with heavy cycle wear typical, and many were replaced by the backwards-compatible 41Ah upgrade, so surviving takeoffs price near salvage. No sold comps found; pegged very conservative.

The doubled-range pack announced Nov 2020 (Electrek), standard on 2021+ 72V bikes including the 2023 LTD; catalog_name kept from v1 since the 41Ah spec already identifies it. Big pack with real replacement cost, tiny buyer pool, unverifiable health. Frame tray width varies by revision (v1.0/v1.2/v1.5) - confirm donor frame version. No sold comps found; pegged very conservative for the pack class.

Pack for the 2024+ RCR 80V (onyxmotors.com). NOT electrically interchangeable with 72V bikes. Newest and highest-value pack in the family but effectively zero resale history; pegged conservative at 40 percent of a cautious comp.
